NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, O LORD, Give ear to my supplications! In Your faithfulness answer me, And in Your righteousness.
NKJV.2: Do not enter into judgment with Your servant,  For in Your sight no one living is righteous.
NKJV.3: For the enemy has persecuted my soul; He has crushed my life to the ground; He has made me dwell in darkness, Like those who have long been dead.
NKJV.4: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me; My heart within me is distressed.
NKJV.5: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all Your works; I muse on the work of Your hands.
NKJV.6: I spread out my hands to You; My soul longs for You like a thirsty land. Selah
NKJV.7: Answer me speedily, O LORD; My spirit fails! Do not hide Your face from me,  Lest I be like those who go down into the pit.
NKJV.8: Cause me to hear Your lovingkindness in the morning, For in You do I trust;  Cause me to know the way in which I should walk, For I lift up my soul to You.
NKJV.9: Deliver me, O LORD, from my enemies; In You I take shelter.
NKJV.10: Teach me to do Your will, For You are my God; Your Spirit is good. Lead me in the land of uprightness.
NKJV.11: Revive me, O LORD, for Your name’s sake! For Your righteousness’ sake bring my soul out of trouble.
NKJV.12: In Your mercy cut off my enemies, And destroy all those who afflict my soul; For I am Your servant.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Hear<S>8085</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, give ear<S>238</S> to my supplications<S>8469</S>: in thy faithfulness<S>530</S> answer<S>6030</S> me, and in thy righteousness<S>6666</S>.
KJV.2: And enter<S>935</S> not into judgment<S>4941</S> with thy servant<S>5650</S>: for in thy sight<S>6440</S> shall no man living<S>2416</S> be justified<S>6663</S>.
KJV.3: For the enemy<S>341</S> hath persecuted<S>7291</S> my soul<S>5315</S>; he hath smitten<S>1792</S> my life<S>2416</S> down<S>1792</S> to the ground<S>776</S>; he hath made me to dwell<S>3427</S> in darkness<S>4285</S>, as those that have been long<S>5769</S> dead<S>4191</S>.
KJV.4: Therefore is my spirit<S>7307</S> overwhelmed<S>5848</S> within me; my heart<S>3820</S> within<S>8432</S> me is desolate<S>8074</S>.
KJV.5: I remember<S>2142</S> the days<S>3117</S> of old<S>6924</S>; I meditate<S>1897</S> on all thy works<S>6467</S>; I muse<S>7878</S> on the work<S>4639</S> of thy hands<S>3027</S>.
KJV.6: I stretch forth<S>6566</S> my hands<S>3027</S> unto thee: my soul<S>5315</S> thirsteth after thee, as a thirsty<S>5889</S> land<S>776</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.7: Hear<S>6030</S> me speedily<S>4118</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>: my spirit<S>7307</S> faileth<S>3615</S>: hide<S>5641</S> not thy face<S>6440</S> from me, lest I be like<S>4911</S> unto them that go down<S>3381</S> into the pit<S>953</S>. <sup>lest: or, for I am become like, etc</sup>
KJV.8: Cause me to hear<S>8085</S> thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S> in the morning<S>1242</S>; for in thee do I trust<S>982</S>: cause me to know<S>3045</S> the way<S>1870</S> wherein<S>2098</S> I should walk<S>3212</S>; for I lift up<S>5375</S> my soul<S>5315</S> unto thee.
KJV.9: Deliver<S>5337</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S>, from mine enemies<S>341</S>: I flee unto thee to hide<S>3680</S> me. <sup>flee: Heb. hide me with thee</sup>
KJV.10: Teach<S>3925</S> me to do<S>6213</S> thy will<S>7522</S>; for thou art my God<S>430</S>: thy spirit<S>7307</S> is good<S>2896</S>; lead<S>5148</S> me into the land<S>776</S> of uprightness<S>4334</S>.
KJV.11: Quicken<S>2421</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S>, for thy name's<S>8034</S> sake: for thy righteousness'<S>6666</S> sake bring<S>3318</S> my soul<S>5315</S> out of trouble<S>6869</S>.
KJV.12: And of thy mercy<S>2617</S> cut off<S>6789</S> mine enemies<S>341</S>, and destroy<S>6</S> all them that afflict<S>6887</S> my soul<S>5315</S>: for I am thy servant<S>5650</S>.

ESV.1: Hear my prayer, O LORD; give ear to my pleas for mercy! In your faithfulness answer me, in your righteousness!
ESV.2: Enter not into judgment with your servant, for no one living is righteous before you.
ESV.3: For the enemy has pursued my soul; he has crushed my life to the ground; he has made me sit in darkness like those long dead.
ESV.4: Therefore my spirit faints within me; my heart within me is appalled.
ESV.5: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all that you have done; I ponder the work of your hands.
ESV.6: I stretch out my hands to you; my soul thirsts for you like a parched land. Selah
ESV.7: Answer me quickly, O LORD! My spirit fails! Hide not your face from me, lest I be like those who go down to the pit.
ESV.8: Let me hear in the morning of your steadfast love, for in you I trust. Make me know the way I should go, for to you I lift up my soul.
ESV.9: Deliver me from my enemies, O LORD! I have fled to you for refuge.
ESV.10: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God! Let your good Spirit lead me on level ground!
ESV.11: For your name's sake, O LORD, preserve my life! In your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble!
ESV.12: And in your steadfast love you will cut off my enemies, and you will destroy all the adversaries of my soul, for I am your servant.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, O LORD, Give ear to my supplications! Answer me in Your faithfulness, in Your righteousness!
NASB.2: And do not enter into judgment with Your servant, For in Your sight no man living is righteous.
NASB.3: For the enemy has persecuted my soul; He has crushed my life to the ground; He has made me dwell in dark places, like those who have long been dead.
NASB.4: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me; My heart is appalled within me.
NASB.5: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all Your doings; I muse on the work of Your hands.
NASB.6: I stretch out my hands to You; My soul [longs] for You, as a parched land. Selah.
NASB.7: Answer me quickly, O LORD, my spirit fails; Do not hide Your face from me, Or I will become like those who go down to the pit.
NASB.8: Let me hear Your lovingkindness in the morning; For I trust in You; Teach me the way in which I should walk; For to You I lift up my soul.
NASB.9: Deliver me, O LORD, from my enemies; I take refuge in You.
NASB.10: Teach me to do Your will, For You are my God; Let Your good Spirit lead me on level ground.
NASB.11: For the sake of Your name, O LORD, revive me. In Your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble.
NASB.12: And in Your lovingkindness, cut off my enemies And destroy all those who afflict my soul, For I am Your servant.

NLT.1:
Hear my prayer, O LORD;
listen to my plea!
Answer me because you are faithful and righteous.
NLT.2:
Don’t put your servant on trial,
for no one is innocent before you.
NLT.3:
My enemy has chased me.
He has knocked me to the ground
and forces me to live in darkness like those in the grave.
NLT.4:
I am losing all hope;
I am paralyzed with fear.
NLT.5:
I remember the days of old.
I ponder all your great works
and think about what you have done.
NLT.6:
I lift my hands to you in prayer.
I thirst for you as parched land thirsts for rain. Interlude
NLT.7:
Come quickly, LORD, and answer me,
for my depression deepens.
Don’t turn away from me,
or I will die.
NLT.8:
Let me hear of your unfailing love each morning,
for I am trusting you.
Show me where to walk,
for I give myself to you.
NLT.9:
Rescue me from my enemies, LORD;
I run to you to hide me.
NLT.10:
Teach me to do your will,
for you are my God.
May your gracious Spirit lead me forward
on a firm footing.
NLT.11:
For the glory of your name, O LORD, preserve my life.
Because of your faithfulness, bring me out of this distress.
NLT.12:
In your unfailing love, silence all my enemies
and destroy all my foes,
for I am your servant.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. Hear my prayer, Yahweh. Listen to my petitions. In your faithfulness and righteousness, relieve me.
WEB.2: Don’t enter into judgment with your servant, for in your sight no man living is righteous.
WEB.3: For the enemy pursues my soul. He has struck my life down to the ground. He has made me live in dark places, as those who have been long dead.
WEB.4: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me. My heart within me is desolate.
WEB.5: I remember the days of old. I meditate on all your doings. I contemplate the work of your hands.
WEB.6: I spread out my hands to you. My soul thirsts for you, like a parched land. Selah.
WEB.7: Hurry to answer me, Yahweh. My spirit fails. Don’t hide your face from me, so that I don’t become like those who go down into the pit.
WEB.8: Cause me to hear your loving kindness in the morning, for I trust in you. Cause me to know the way in which I should walk, for I lift up my soul to you.
WEB.9: Deliver me, Yahweh, from my enemies. I flee to you to hide me.
WEB.10: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. Your Spirit is good. Lead me in the land of uprightness.
WEB.11: Revive me, Yahweh, for your name’s sake. In your righteousness, bring my soul out of trouble.
WEB.12: In your loving kindness, cut off my enemies, and destroy all those who afflict my soul, For I am your servant.

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, O LORD,
Listen to my supplications!
Answer me in Your faithfulness, and in Your righteousness.
AMP.2:
And do not enter into judgment with Your servant,
For in Your sight no man living is righteous or justified.
AMP.3:
For the enemy has persecuted me,
He has crushed my life down to the ground;
He has made me dwell in dark places, like those who have been long dead.
AMP.4:
Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed and weak within me [wrapped in darkness];
My heart grows numb within me.
AMP.5:
I remember the days of old;
I meditate on all that You have done;
I ponder the work of Your hands.
AMP.6:
I reach out my hands to You;
My throat thirsts for You, as a parched land [thirsts for water]. Selah.
AMP.7:
Answer me quickly, O LORD, my spirit fails;
Do not hide Your face from me,
Or I will become like those who go down into the pit (grave).
AMP.8:
Let me hear Your lovingkindness in the morning,
For I trust in You.
Teach me the way in which I should walk,
For I lift up my soul to You.
AMP.9:
Rescue me, O LORD, from my enemies;
I take refuge in You.
AMP.10:
Teach me to do Your will [so that I may please You],
For You are my God;
Let Your good Spirit lead me on level ground.
AMP.11:
Save my life, O LORD, for Your name's sake;
In Your righteousness bring my life out of trouble.
AMP.12:
In your lovingkindness, silence and destroy my enemies
And destroy all those who afflict my life,
For I am Your servant.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Listen to this prayer of mine, God;
pay attention to what I'm asking.
Answer me—you're famous for your answers!
Do what's right for me.
MSG.2:
But don't, please don't, haul me into court;
not a person alive would be acquitted there.
MSG.3:
The enemy hunted me down;
he kicked me and stomped me within an inch of my life.
He put me in a black hole,
buried me like a corpse in that dungeon.
MSG.4:
I sat there in despair, my spirit draining away,
my heart heavy, like lead.
MSG.5:
I remembered the old days,
went over all you've done, pondered the ways you've worked,
MSG.6:
Stretched out my hands to you,
as thirsty for you as a desert thirsty for rain.
MSG.7:
Hurry with your answer, God!
I'm nearly at the end of my rope.
Don't turn away; don't ignore me!
That would be certain death.
MSG.8:
If you wake me each morning with the sound of your loving voice,
I'll go to sleep each night trusting in you.
Point out the road I must travel;
I'm all ears, all eyes before you.
MSG.9:
Save me from my enemies, God—
you're my only hope!
MSG.10:
Teach me how to live to please you,
because you're my God.
Lead me by your blessed Spirit
into cleared and level pastureland.
MSG.11:
Keep up your reputation, God—give me life!
In your justice, get me out of this trouble!
MSG.12:
In your great love, vanquish my enemies;
make a clean sweep of those who harass me.
And why? Because I'm your servant.

NIV.1:
Psalm 143
A psalm of David.
O Lord, hear my prayer,
listen to my cry for mercy;
in your faithfulness and righteousness
come to my relief.
NIV.2:
Do not bring your servant into judgment,
for no one living is righteous before you.
NIV.3:
The enemy pursues me,
he crushes me to the ground;
he makes me dwell in darkness
like those long dead.
NIV.4:
So my spirit grows faint within me;
my heart within me is dismayed.
NIV.5:
I remember the days of long ago;
I meditate on all your works
and consider what your hands have done.
NIV.6:
I spread out my hands to you;
my soul thirsts for you like a parched land. Selah
NIV.7:
Answer me quickly, O Lord;
my spirit fails.
Do not hide your face from me
or I will be like those who go down to the pit.
NIV.8:
Let the morning bring me word of your unfailing love,
for I have put my trust in you.
Show me the way I should go,
for to you I lift up my soul.
NIV.9:
Rescue me from my enemies, O Lord,
for I hide myself in you.
NIV.10:
Teach me to do your will,
for you are my God;
may your good Spirit
lead me on level ground.
NIV.11:
For your name’s sake, O Lord, preserve my life;
in your righteousness, bring me out of trouble.
NIV.12:
In your unfailing love, silence my enemies;
destroy all my foes,
for I am your servant.

NRSVCE.1:
Hear my prayer, O LORD;
give ear to my supplications in your faithfulness;
answer me in your righteousness.
NRSVCE.2:
Do not enter into judgment with your servant,
for no one living is righteous before you.
NRSVCE.3:
For the enemy has pursued me,
crushing my life to the ground,
making me sit in darkness like those long dead.
NRSVCE.4:
Therefore my spirit faints within me;
my heart within me is appalled.
NRSVCE.5:
I remember the days of old,
I think about all your deeds,
I meditate on the works of your hands.
NRSVCE.6:
I stretch out my hands to you;
my soul thirsts for you like a parched land. Selah
NRSVCE.7:
Answer me quickly, O LORD;
my spirit fails.
Do not hide your face from me,
or I shall be like those who go down to the Pit.
NRSVCE.8:
Let me hear of your steadfast love in the morning,
for in you I put my trust.
Teach me the way I should go,
for to you I lift up my soul.
NRSVCE.9:
Save me, O LORD, from my enemies;
I have fled to you for refuge.
NRSVCE.10:
Teach me to do your will,
for you are my God.
Let your good spirit lead me
on a level path.
NRSVCE.11:
For your name’s sake, O LORD, preserve my life.
In your righteousness bring me out of trouble.
NRSVCE.12:
In your steadfast love cut off my enemies,
and destroy all my adversaries,
for I am your servant.

NET.1: O LORD, hear my prayer! Pay attention to my plea for help! Because of your faithfulness and justice, answer me!
NET.2: Do not sit in judgment on your servant, for no one alive is innocent before you.
NET.3: Certainly my enemies chase me. They smash me into the ground. They force me to live in dark regions, like those who have been dead for ages.
NET.4: My strength leaves me; I am absolutely shocked.
NET.5: I recall the old days; I meditate on all you have done; I reflect on your accomplishments.
NET.6: I spread my hands out to you in prayer; my soul thirsts for you in a parched land.
NET.7: Answer me quickly, LORD! My strength is fading. Do not reject me, or I will join those descending into the grave.
NET.8: May I hear about your loyal love in the morning, for I trust in you. Show me the way I should go, because I long for you.
NET.9: Rescue me from my enemies, O LORD! I run to you for protection.
NET.10: Teach me to do what pleases you, for you are my God. May your kind presence lead me into a level land.
NET.11: O LORD, for the sake of your reputation, revive me! Because of your justice, rescue me from trouble!
NET.12: As a demonstration of your loyal love, destroy my enemies! Annihilate all who threaten my life, for I am your servant.
